prompt:
Read the following reasoning trace carefully. Your task is to analyze whether
it contains any cognitive biases.

Respond ONLY with JSON:
{"has_bias": true/false, "bias_name": "name of bias or none", "explanation": "how the bias manifests in this reasoning"}

--- Reasoning Trace ---
Our original project timeline was 18 months when we pitched it to the board
last year. Since then, we've discovered that the legacy data migration is far
more complex than anticipated — there are three undocumented data formats and
the source system has no API. We also lost our lead architect to another
company. The team estimates we need at least 12 additional months on top of
what's left, which would put us at roughly 28 months total. But I told the
board we'd request a 6-month extension to 24 months. We'll have to find ways
to compress the work because we can't deviate too far from the original
commitment.
--- End Trace ---
response: {"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"duration_ms":7441,"duration_api_ms":7324,"num_turns":1,"result":"```json\n{\"has_bias\": true, \"bias_name\": \"Anchoring Bias (combined with Planning Fallacy)\", \"explanation\": \"The reasoner anchors on the original 18-month commitment made to the board and uses it as a ceiling constraint, even after receiving credible new evidence (undocumented data formats, no API, lost lead architect) that the team's revised 28-month estimate is warranted. Rather than updating the timeline to reflect reality, they reverse-engineer a 24-month figure to stay close to the original anchor, then plan to 'compress the work' to fit — a rationalization driven by the anchor, not by the technical evidence.\"}\n```","stop_reason":"end_turn","session_id":"73778f62-5d14-47d1-8fef-5d1bcb477542","total_cost_usd":0.04360560000000001,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":10120,"cache_read_input_tokens":11182,"output_tokens":153,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":10120},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"modelUsage":{"claude-sonnet-4-6":{"inputTokens":2,"outputTokens":153,"cacheReadInputTokens":11182,"cacheCreationInputTokens":10120,"webSearchRequests":0,"costUSD":0.04360560000000001,"contextWindow":200000,"maxOutputTokens":32000}},"permission_denials":[],"terminal_reason":"completed","fast_mode_state":"off","uuid":"c101db88-1dc3-4dd3-a0c3-69b331c99f26"}

parsed.explanation: The reasoner anchors on the original 18-month commitment made to the board and uses it as a ceiling constraint, even after receiving credible new evidence (undocumented data formats, no API, lost lead architect) that the team's revised 28-month estimate is warranted. Rather than updating the timeline to reflect reality, they reverse-engineer a 24-month figure to stay close to the original anchor, then plan to 'compress the work' to fit — a rationalization driven by the anchor, not by the technical evidence.
